expectations.text: Recommends writing an ADR explaining the UserRepository requirement
expectations.evidence: Layer 2 is a full ADR template: docs/decisions/0005-user-repository-required.md

expectations.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Layer 1 is CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with a Security Constraints section and full example

expectations.text: States the positive alternative (use UserRepository, not just prohibition)
expectations.evidence: 'The positive alternative is given immediately (use UserRepository instead) — rules without alternatives are less effective'

expectations.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools
expectations.evidence: 'An ADR prevents the constraint from being improved away. Without an ADR, a future engineer (or AI) might look at UserRepository, decide it is an unnecessary abstraction, and refactor around it'

expectations.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism
expectations.evidence: Layer 3: UserRepository Javadoc docstring and users table migration comment; Layer 4: subdirectory CLAUDE.md

expectations.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly
expectations.evidence: 'Human engineers and AI tools find documentation through different paths' — both addressed throughout all layers
